title: Replace local Maia-2 engine with remote Maia3 API
description:
Replace the entire local Maia-2 inference + Stockfish calibration pipeline in
opponent_move_controller.py with a single HTTP call to the maiachess.com Maia3 API.

## API Details

Endpoint: POST https://www.maiachess.com/api/v1/play/get_move
Query params: maia_name, initial_clock=0, current_clock=0, maia_version=maia3
Content-Type: application/json
Body: JSON array of UCI move strings from game start, e.g. ["e2e4","e7e6","d2d4"]
Response: {"top_move":"e4d2","move_delay":0.0}

Available maia_name values (each represents a different ELO difficulty):
maia_kdd_600, maia_kdd_800, maia_kdd_1000, maia_kdd_1100, maia_kdd_1200,
maia_kdd_1300, maia_kdd_1400, maia_kdd_1500, maia_kdd_1600, maia_kdd_1700,
maia_kdd_1800, maia_kdd_1900, maia_kdd_2000, maia_kdd_2200, maia_kdd_2400,
maia_kdd_2600

## Key Design Decision: Frontend sends move list (Option A)

The Maia3 API requires the full game move history as UCI strings, not a FEN.
We chose to have the frontend send `moves: string[]` in the request rather than
storing move history server-side because:
- Minimal backend change (no DB migration needed)
- Stateless server (no sync issues)
- Frontend already tracks moveHistory in ChessGame.tsx state
- Server-side option has a "reconstruction problem" — server only receives FEN,
  not the player's move as UCI, so it would need an API change anyway

## Implementation Plan

### Frontend changes
1. Track UCI moves alongside existing MoveRecord {san, fen} in ChessGame.tsx.
   MoveRecord currently lacks UCI — either add a `uci` field or reconstruct from
   chess.js move result ({from, to, promotion}).
2. Add `moves: string[]` param to getNextOpponentMove() in src/utils/api.ts
3. Pass accumulated UCI move list from useOpponentMove.ts hook
4. Update NextOpponentMoveRequest call sites to include moves array

### Backend changes
1. Add `moves: list[str]` field to NextOpponentMoveRequest Pydantic model
2. Rewrite choose_move() in opponent_move_controller.py:
   - Accept moves list instead of (or in addition to) FEN
   - Map target_elo → nearest maia_name bin
   - POST to Maia3 API with the move list
   - Parse top_move from response
   - Convert UCI → SAN using python-chess Board (still a dependency)
   - Return ControllerMove(uci, san, method="maia3_api")
3. Remove/deprecate: MaiaEngineService, StockfishService, loss_distribution,
   VMU_ENABLED flag, MAIA_ELO_FLOOR, local maia2 model files (~500MB)

### ELO → maia_name mapping
Simple nearest-bin lookup:
  bins = [600,800,1000,1100,1200,1300,1400,1500,1600,1700,1800,1900,2000,2200,2400,2600]
  nearest = min(bins, key=lambda b: abs(b - elo))
  maia_name = f"maia_kdd_{nearest}"

## Trade-offs
- Latency: ~50ms local → ~200-500ms network round-trip
- Reliability: self-contained → depends on maiachess.com uptime
- Rate limits: none → unknown/undocumented
- Probability distribution: full candidate list → single top_move only
- Sub-1100 play: Stockfish calibration → native Maia3 600/800/1000 models (simpler)
- Model files: ~500MB on disk → none needed
- TOS: N/A → using an undocumented external API

## Files affected
- backend/app/opponent_move_controller.py (rewrite)
- backend/app/api/game.py (add moves field to request model)
- src/components/ChessGame.tsx (track UCI in MoveRecord)
- src/utils/api.ts (add moves param)
- src/hooks/useOpponentMove.ts (pass moves to API call)

title: Consolidate in-game messaging into MoveList cells
description:
Move all in-game position/move messaging out of floating toasts and into the MoveList component, attached to the relevant move cell.

**What to consolidate:**
- Blunder warnings (currently a floating toast)
- Position reviews
- SRS pass/fail indicators

**Desired UX:**
When an event occurs (e.g. blunder detected), the corresponding move cell in the MoveList receives an inline pop-up, badge, or other noticeable indicator showing there is information about that move. Clicking or hovering reveals details.

**Goal:** All move-specific feedback lives spatially attached to the move that triggered it, replacing disconnected floating toasts.
design:
## Message Display Rules

### Data Model
- Replace single `bubble` prop with `Map<number, MoveMessage[]>` keyed by move index
- Each `MoveMessage` has: type (analyzing, blunder, inaccuracy, good, great, best, srs-pass, srs-fail), text, optional detail
- Messages accumulate per move — new events append, nothing overwritten

### Layout Rules (3-column grid: number | white | black)

**Case 1 — White move has message:**
- Row: `[3] [Nf3] [...]` (ellipsis placeholder in black cell)
- Full-width message row below
- Next row: `[..] [—] [e6]` (black move shifted down)

**Case 2 — Black move has message:**
- Row: `[3] [Nf3] [e6]` (pair stays together)
- Full-width message row below
- The filled black cell is the visual indicator that the message belongs to black

**Case 3 — Both moves have messages:**
- Row: `[3] [Nf3] [...]`
- White's message row
- Row: `[..] [—] [e6]`
- Black's message row

### Message Styling
- Message title text color matches the move classification color (blunder=red, good=green, etc.)
- Messages are expanded by default (no click-to-reveal)
- Full-width messages span all 3 grid columns
- Multiple messages on one move stack vertically

### Analyzing Indicator
- Replace the analyzing-variant bubble with a small inline spinner next to the move SAN
- No full-width row needed for the analyzing state

### Persistent Indicators
- Classification dots/icons persist next to moves after analysis
- Detail text always visible below the relevant move cell
notes:
## Existing infrastructure

A `moveBubble` mechanism already exists:
- `ChessGame.tsx` computes a `MoveListBubble` via `useMemo` (~line 436) based on analysis state (`isAnalyzing`, `analyzingMove`, `lastAnalysis`, `isPlayerMoveIndex`)
- `MoveList.tsx` receives it as an optional `bubble` prop and renders it inline below the relevant move pair row
- Bubble variants: `analyzing`, `blunder`, `inaccuracy`, `good`, `great`, `best`
- This is a good foundation to build on — no need to create a new rendering path

## Design change: analyzing indicator

Replace the text bubble `"Analyzing Nf3…"` with a small spinning icon rendered **next to the move cell** itself (not as a full-width bubble row). The spinner should appear inline in the move's row, keeping the UI compact. Remove the analyzing-variant bubble in favor of this per-cell spinner.
close_reason: Already fully implemented: MoveMessage type, Map<number, MoveMessage[]> data model, appendMoveMessage, MoveRow rendering, AnalysisConnectors integration all in place. Old moveBubble system removed.

title: [SPEC] Clarify transposition detection and ghost re-hooking
description:
**Category: SPEC.md Update Required**

Section 2.1 mentions 'Re-Hooking' when user transposes into a known position, but the mechanism isn't specified.

## The Scenario
1. Move 5: User deviates from ghost path
2. Engine mode takes over
3. Move 12: Position happens to match a blunder path
4. Ghost should reactivate

## Questions

### When to Check?
- Every move? (more API calls)
- Only on 'significant' moves?

### What Triggers Re-Hook?
- Any position in the graph?
- Only positions with due blunders downstream?

## Proposed SPEC.md Addition

### Section 6.1.1: Re-Hooking Logic
After every user move, frontend calls GET /api/game/ghost-move with current FEN.

Backend logic:
1. Look up position by normalized FEN hash
2. If not found → return { mode: 'engine' }
3. If found, run downstream blunder query
4. If due blunder reachable → return { mode: 'ghost', move: ... }
5. Otherwise → return { mode: 'engine' }

### Performance Note
This check happens every move. Response time target: <100ms.
Consider caching position lookups.
close_reason: Added section 6.1.1 Re-Hooking Logic to SPEC.md clarifying transposition detection mechanism

title: Choose frontend state management solution
description:
The spec says 'React + Vite' but doesn't specify state management.

## State to Manage

### Game State
- Current FEN position
- Move history
- Game mode (ghost vs engine)
- Clock times (if implemented)
- Whose turn

### UI State
- Selected square
- Legal moves highlighting
- Promotion dialog
- Analysis panel open/closed

### User State
- Auth token
- User profile
- Preferences (engine level, board theme)

### Server State
- Blunder list
- Stats data
- Due reviews count

## Options

### 1. React Context + useReducer
- Built-in, no dependencies
- Good for simple apps
- Pros: No extra bundle size, familiar
- Cons: Can get messy at scale, no devtools

### 2. Zustand
- Minimal, hooks-based
- ~1KB bundle
- Pros: Simple API, good devtools, no boilerplate
- Cons: Less structure than Redux

### 3. Redux Toolkit
- Full-featured, battle-tested
- ~10KB bundle
- Pros: Great devtools, middleware, time-travel debugging
- Cons: More boilerplate, overkill for MVP?

### 4. Jotai / Recoil
- Atomic state model
- Good for granular updates
- Pros: Fine-grained reactivity
- Cons: Different mental model, smaller community

### 5. TanStack Query (React Query)
- Server state management
- Not for local state
- Pros: Caching, refetching, loading states
- Cons: Only for async data

## Recommendation

**Zustand for local/game state + TanStack Query for server state**

```typescript
// Game store (Zustand)
const useGameStore = create((set) => ({
  fen: STARTING_FEN,
  mode: 'engine',
  makeMove: (move) => set((state) => ({ fen: applyMove(state.fen, move) })),
}));

// Server data (TanStack Query)
const { data: stats } = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['stats'],
  queryFn: fetchStats,
});
```

## Implementation
- Create /src/stores/ directory
- Separate stores by domain: gameStore, uiStore, authStore
- Use TanStack Query for all API calls
- Document state shape and actions
close_reason: Reorganizing into spec vs implementation categories

title: splashy analysis result
description:
After a move is played by the user add some kind of splashy or sparkly animation around the move icon when the analysis results become available to draw attention to it.

When the result is a Best move make it even more special and include a bling sound. This only applies to user moves, engine moves analysis results get no animation.

## Chosen approach: Pop & Glow

When analysis arrives for a user move, the classification icon does a scale-bounce + glow entrance:

- **All classifications**: Icon scales from 0 → 1.2 → 1.0 over ~400ms with a brief color-matched glow (box-shadow pulse). A CSS class `move-icon--just-arrived` triggers the keyframe, removed after animation ends.
- **Best move upgrade**: Bounce is bigger (0 → 1.5 → 1.0), glow is gold/blue, and a second ring of sparkle radiates outward using a pseudo-element with a radial-gradient that fades. Play a short bling sound.
- **Engine moves**: No animation at all — only user moves get the treatment.
design:
# Pop & Glow — Implementation Plan (v3.1)

## Overview

When analysis completes for a **user move during live gameplay**, the classification icon animates in with a scale-bounce and color-matched glow. Best moves get an enhanced animation plus a bling sound. Engine moves and analysis-board moves show their icon instantly with no animation.

"During gameplay" means: animate only when a fresh result arrives for the first time in the current session. Previously-resolved results that are merely redisplayed (e.g. on remount) do NOT animate.

---

## 1. Track freshly-resolved player move indices in the analysis store

**File:** `src/stores/createAnalysisStore.ts`

Add a `freshlyResolved: Set<number>` field to the store state:

```ts
// New state field:
freshlyResolved: Set<number>

// New actions:
markFreshlyResolved: (moveIndex: number) => void
clearFreshlyResolved: (moveIndex: number) => void
```

- `markFreshlyResolved` adds an index to the set.
- `clearFreshlyResolved` removes a single index.
- `resolveAnalysis` does NOT touch `freshlyResolved`. Callers decide what to mark.
- `resetTransient` (line 55) clears `freshlyResolved` — remounts/worker restarts don't strand flags.
- `clearAll` (line 64) clears `freshlyResolved` — new game starts clean.

---

## 2. Mark fresh indices from ConnectedMoveList, driven by lastAnalysis

**File:** `src/components/chess-game/AnalysisConnectors.tsx`

**Why lastAnalysis, not analysisMap diffing:** The singleton store (`gameAnalysisStore`, line 76 of createAnalysisStore.ts) preserves `analysisMap` across remounts via `resetTransient` (called in useMoveAnalysis.ts:183). If we diff the map, the first mount would see every existing entry as "new" and re-animate all of them. `lastAnalysis` is transient — it's cleared by `resetTransient` — so it only fires as an event for genuinely new arrivals.

ConnectedMoveList already has `isPlayerMoveIndex` (line 179). Add:

```ts
const lastAnalysis = useAnalysisStore((s) => s.lastAnalysis);

useEffect(() => {
  if (
    !lastAnalysis ||
    lastAnalysis.moveIndex === null ||
    !lastAnalysis.classification ||
    !isPlayerMoveIndex(lastAnalysis.moveIndex)
  ) {
    return;
  }
  analysisStoreApi.getState().markFreshlyResolved(lastAnalysis.moveIndex);
}, [lastAnalysis, isPlayerMoveIndex, analysisStoreApi]);
```

**Why gate on `classification !== null`:** `classification` is nullable in the live pipeline — `classifyMove` returns null when delta is null (analysisUtils.ts:141), and the worker can emit that path (analysisWorker.ts:233). MoveRow only renders an icon when classification exists (MoveRow.tsx:154). A player result with `classification: null` would enter `freshlyResolved`, never render an animating icon, never fire `animationEnd`, and sit in the set until reset. Gating here ensures every entry in `freshlyResolved` will produce an icon that can clean itself up.

This means:
- Only gameplay player moves **with a non-null classification** get marked.
- Engine moves are never marked (not player moves).
- Results with `classification: null` are never marked (no icon to animate).
- AnalysisBoard uses `MoveList` directly (line 654 of AnalysisBoard.tsx), not `ConnectedMoveList`, so no animation in analysis views.
- On remount, `resetTransient` nulls `lastAnalysis`, so the effect doesn't fire for stale data.
- On new game, `clearAll` nulls `lastAnalysis` and empties `freshlyResolved`.

**No timeout fallback needed.** MoveList is not virtualized — all rows are always rendered in the DOM. `onAnimationEnd` will reliably fire for every animated icon. The only bulk unmount is game reset, which calls `clearAll`.

---

## 3. Thread freshlyResolved through MoveList → MoveRow

**File:** `src/components/MoveList.tsx`

- Add prop `freshlyResolvedIndices?: ReadonlySet<number>`
- Add prop `onFreshAnimationDone?: (index: number) => void`
- Pass to each MoveRow as two booleans: `freshWhite` and `freshBlack`, plus the callback.

**File:** `src/components/MoveRow.tsx`

- Add props `freshWhite: boolean`, `freshBlack: boolean`, `onFreshAnimationDone?: (index: number) => void`
- In `renderMoveCell`, when `iconInfo` exists AND fresh is true for this side:
  - Add class `move-icon--pop` (or `move-icon--pop-best` if classification === 'best')
  - Attach `onAnimationEnd={() => onFreshAnimationDone?.(index)}`
- Update `areEqual` to compare `freshWhite`/`freshBlack`

**Wire-up in ConnectedMoveList** (`src/components/chess-game/AnalysisConnectors.tsx`):
- Subscribe to `freshlyResolved` from the analysis store.
- Pass as `freshlyResolvedIndices` to MoveList.
- Provide `onFreshAnimationDone` that calls `analysisStoreApi.getState().clearFreshlyResolved(moveIndex)`.

---

## 4. CSS keyframes — scale-bounce with sizing constraints

**File:** `src/App.css`

### Constraint analysis

`.move-button` has `overflow: hidden` (line 2167) with `padding: 0.35rem 0.3rem` (line 2161). The icon at rest is ~16px wide (1.15em best-move variant inside 0.9rem button font). Available padding before clipping: ~4.8px per side.

- **scale(1.25):** icon grows ~2px per side → fits within padding. Safe.
- **scale(1.35):** icon grows ~2.8px per side → within 4.8px budget. Safe.
- **box-shadow:** will be clipped by `overflow: hidden` on `.move-button`. To allow the best-move glow to be visible, the animation class adds `overflow: visible` on the icon's nearest clipping ancestor (`.move-button`) while the animation is active.

```css
/* Standard pop entrance for all non-best classifications */
@keyframes move-icon-pop {
  0%   { transform: scale(0); opacity: 0; }
  60%  { transform: scale(1.25); opacity: 1; }
  100% { transform: scale(1); }
}

/* Enhanced pop for best move — bigger bounce + gold glow via box-shadow */
@keyframes move-icon-pop-best {
  0%   { transform: scale(0); opacity: 0;
         box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(255, 215, 0, 0); }
  50%  { transform: scale(1.35); opacity: 1;
         box-shadow: 0 0 8px 2px rgba(255, 215, 0, 0.45); }
  80%  { box-shadow: 0 0 4px 1px rgba(255, 215, 0, 0.15); }
  100% { transform: scale(1);
         box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(255, 215, 0, 0); }
}

.move-icon--pop {
  animation: move-icon-pop 400ms ease-out both;
}

.move-icon--pop-best {
  animation: move-icon-pop-best 500ms ease-out both;
}

/* Allow box-shadow glow to escape the button during best-move animation.
   The button's overflow:hidden (line 2167) clips box-shadow by default.
   This override is scoped to buttons that contain an animating best-move icon. */
.move-button:has(.move-icon--pop-best) {
  overflow: visible;
}
```

For the non-best pop, no overflow change is needed — scale(1.25) fits within padding, and there's no box-shadow to clip.

---

## 5. Bling sound in AnalysisEffects

**File:** `src/utils/blingSound.ts` (new, ~25 lines)

Synthesize a short bling via Web Audio API:
- Lazy `AudioContext` creation. Graceful failure via `.catch(() => {})`, matching the existing blunder audio pattern at `AnalysisEffects.tsx:50`.
- Two quick sine tones: C6 (1047Hz, 80ms) → E6 (1319Hz, 120ms) with fast exponential decay.
- Export `playBling()`.

**File:** `src/components/chess-game/AnalysisEffects.tsx`

Add a new `useEffect` that watches `lastAnalysis`. When:
1. `lastAnalysis` is non-null, AND
2. `lastAnalysis.moveIndex` is a player move (via existing `isPlayerMoveIndex`, line 66), AND
3. `lastAnalysis.classification === 'best'`

…call `playBling()`.

This follows the existing pattern: blunder audio is already triggered here (line 259 `playRandomBlunderAudio()`). Centralizing sound in AnalysisEffects avoids coupling to MoveRow mount/remount behavior and prevents duplicate playback.

---

## 6. Edge cases

- **Rapid analysis completions:** Multiple icons animate simultaneously — fine, each has its own animation instance.
- **Remount with prepopulated analysisMap:** `resetTransient` nulls `lastAnalysis` and clears `freshlyResolved`. The `lastAnalysis` effect in ConnectedMoveList won't fire. No re-animation.
- **New game / clearAll:** Clears `freshlyResolved`, nulls `lastAnalysis`. Move indices restart at 0. No stale state carries over because both the set and the event signal are wiped.
- **Null classification:** Player results with `classification: null` are not marked fresh (no icon to animate, no `animationEnd` to clean up). No stale entries.
- **AnalysisBoard:** Uses `MoveList` directly (not `ConnectedMoveList`), never receives `freshlyResolvedIndices` — icons appear instantly, no animation.
- **`:has()` browser support:** Supported in all evergreen browsers (Chrome 105+, Firefox 121+, Safari 15.4+). Acceptable for this project.

---

## 7. Testing

### Store tests (`createAnalysisStore.test.ts` or inline)
- `markFreshlyResolved` adds index to set
- `clearFreshlyResolved` removes index
- `resetTransient` clears the set
- `clearAll` clears the set

### ConnectedMoveList / connector tests (`src/components/chess-game/AnalysisConnectors.test.tsx`)
- When `lastAnalysis` arrives with a player-move index and non-null classification, `markFreshlyResolved` is called
- When `lastAnalysis` arrives with an engine-move index, `markFreshlyResolved` is NOT called
- When `lastAnalysis` arrives with a player-move index but `classification: null`, `markFreshlyResolved` is NOT called
- **Lifecycle: remount with prepopulated analysisMap** — verify no indices are marked fresh (lastAnalysis is null after resetTransient)
- Verify `freshlyResolvedIndices` and `onFreshAnimationDone` are passed to MoveList

### MoveRow tests (`MoveRow.test.tsx` or inline)
- `freshWhite=true` + classification='best' → `move-icon--pop-best` class present
- `freshWhite=true` + classification='good' → `move-icon--pop` class present
- `freshWhite=false` → no pop class
- `onAnimationEnd` fires → `onFreshAnimationDone` called with correct index

### AnalysisEffects tests
- `lastAnalysis` with classification='best' + player move → `playBling` called
- `lastAnalysis` with classification='best' + engine move → `playBling` NOT called
- `lastAnalysis` with classification='good' + player move → `playBling` NOT called

---

## File change summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/stores/createAnalysisStore.ts` | Add `freshlyResolved` set + `markFreshlyResolved`/`clearFreshlyResolved` actions; reset in `resetTransient` and `clearAll` |
| `src/components/chess-game/AnalysisConnectors.tsx` | `lastAnalysis`-driven effect to mark player-move indices fresh (gated on non-null classification); subscribe to `freshlyResolved`; pass to MoveList + provide `onFreshAnimationDone` |
| `src/components/MoveList.tsx` | Add `freshlyResolvedIndices`/`onFreshAnimationDone` props; thread as booleans to MoveRow |
| `src/components/MoveRow.tsx` | Add `freshWhite`/`freshBlack`/`onFreshAnimationDone` props; conditional pop classes; `onAnimationEnd` handler; update `areEqual` |
| `src/components/chess-game/AnalysisEffects.tsx` | New `useEffect` for best-move bling (mirrors blunder audio pattern) |
| `src/App.css` | Add `move-icon-pop` and `move-icon-pop-best` keyframes + classes; `:has()`-scoped overflow override on `.move-button` for best-move glow |
| `src/utils/blingSound.ts` | New ~25-line file: Web Audio bling synthesizer with graceful failure |

title: [SPEC] Define blunder context storage schema
description:
**Category: SPEC.md Update Required**

The current schema marks positions as blunders but doesn't store what the mistake was.

## Problem
To show 'You played Qxd4, but Nf3 was better' we need:
- The user's move
- The engine's best move  
- The evaluation difference

## Proposed SPEC.md Addition

### Section 5.3: `blunders` Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE blunders (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id BIGINT REFERENCES users(id),
    position_id BIGINT REFERENCES positions(id),
    user_move_san VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    best_move_san VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    eval_delta INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- centipawns lost
    -- SRS fields (moved from positions)
    srs_due_date TIMESTAMP,
    srs_interval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    srs_ease_factor FLOAT DEFAULT 2.5,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Design Decision
This separates the graph structure (positions/moves) from user-specific blunder tracking. Same position can be a blunder for multiple users with different SRS schedules.
close_reason: Updated SPEC.md with blunder context storage: added blunder_move_san for display, clarified that pass/fail uses real-time eval (not stored moves), any blunder at position = fail

title: Add on-demand opening stats API
description: Expose the single-root opening calculator through dedicated stats endpoints instead of folding it into the existing summary payload. Start with on-demand backend responses for one opening root, including the score payload needed for validation and debugging.
design:
# Plan: g-opening-api — On-demand opening stats API

## Context

The opening score calculator (`opening_rootcalc.py`) and its dependencies (graph, evidence overlay, roots) are fully implemented and tested but not exposed via any API endpoint. The `g-opening-api` task creates the first HTTP interface so the frontend (and debugging tools) can request scores for individual opening roots on demand. This directly unblocks `g-opening-cache` (which wraps this API with a cache layer and batch computation). The `g-opening-family-ui` task depends on *both* this and `g-opening-cache` — it needs the batch/family-level aggregation that the cache layer provides, not just single-root on-demand requests.

## Endpoints

### 1. `POST /api/openings/score`

Computes the score for a single opening root. Authenticated (standard middleware, no security.py changes).

**Why POST**: `opening_key` is a normalized FEN (`rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/4P3/8/PPPP1PPP/RNBQKBNR b KQkq -`) with slashes and spaces — unusable as a query param.

**Request body:**
```json
{ "opening_key": "<normalized FEN>", "player_color": "white" | "black" }
```

**Query param:** `debug: bool = False` — when true, includes per-node debug output for formula tuning.

**Response:** mirrors `RootScore` dataclass fields (score, confidence, coverage, weighted_depth, sample_size, branches, etc.). `debug_nodes` is `[]` unless `?debug=true`.

**Data flow:**
```
request → get_opening_graph() [singleton] + get_opening_roots() [singleton]
        → roots.get_root(key) or 404
        → overlay_evidence(db, user_id, player_color, graph)  [3 SQL queries]
        → compute_root_score(key, color, graph, overlay, roots, debug)
        → serialize RootScore → JSON response
```

### 2. `GET /api/openings/roots`

Lists available opening roots grouped by family. Authenticated (same as all `/api/*` routes — the middleware auth-gates the entire prefix). The data is not user-specific (it comes from the opening graph singletons, not the DB), but auth is required by the existing middleware contract and we don't change that.

**Query param:** `family: str | None` — optional filter to a single family name.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "families": [{ "family_name": "...", "roots": [{ "opening_key", "opening_name", "opening_family", "eco", "depth" }] }],
  "total_roots": 150,
  "total_families": 25
}
```

## Files

### Create: `backend/app/api/openings.py`
- Pydantic models: `RootScoreRequest`, `RootScoreResponse`, `BranchSummaryResponse`, `NodeDebugResponse`, `OpeningRootItem`, `OpeningFamilyItem`, `OpeningRootsListResponse`
- Validation: `player_color` must be "white" or "black"
- Conversion helpers: dataclass → Pydantic (use `dataclasses.fields()` since `NodeDebug` uses `slots=True`)
- Router with both endpoints
- Imports: `get_opening_graph`, `get_opening_roots`, `overlay_evidence`, `compute_root_score`

### Modify: `backend/app/main.py`
- Import and register `openings_router` (2 lines, same pattern as existing 9 routers)

### Create: `backend/test_openings_api.py`
- Mock `get_opening_graph` and `get_opening_roots` singletons with small test fixtures (reuse `_make_node`/`_make_root` patterns from `test_opening_rootcalc.py`)
- Mock `overlay_evidence` to avoid real DB schema dependencies
- Test cases:
  1. Unknown root → 404
  2. Valid root, no evidence → correct structure + prior-based score
  3. Valid root with evidence → score changes
  4. `?debug=true` → non-empty `debug_nodes`
  5. Invalid player_color → 422
  6. `GET /roots` → correct family/root listing
  7. `GET /roots?family=X` → filtered result
  8. No auth on `/score` → 401
  9. No auth on `/roots` → 401

## Verification
```bash
cd backend && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest test_openings_api.py -v
```

title: Add multi-opening cache and recompute
description: Persist per-user, per-color opening root-score snapshots with explicit batch semantics, add rollout backfill plus first-read bootstrap for historical evidence, and refresh snapshots after session upload and SRS review. Keep background recompute as an optional later refinement, but preserve a single shared refresh path for future imported-game ingestion.
design:
## Plan: g-opening-cache — Cached root-score snapshots, recompute, and bootstrap

### Goal

Persist per-user, per-color opening root scores so read APIs can serve cached data without calling `overlay_evidence()` and `compute_root_score()` on demand for every root.

This issue owns:
- cache schema
- recompute flow
- snapshot semantics
- rollout backfill for pre-cache historical data
- first-read bootstrap behavior

Downstream consumers:
- `g-opening-family-api`
- `g-opening-drill-api`
- `g-opening-family-ui`

### Mandatory snapshot contract

Snapshot coherence is not optional.

A read for one `(user_id, player_color)` must select exactly one complete recompute batch. Read APIs must never mix old and new root rows for the same user/color.

Implement this with explicit batch metadata.

Recommended schema:

```python
class OpeningScoreBatch:
    id: int
    user_id: int
    player_color: str
    computed_at: datetime

class UserOpeningScore:
    id: int
    batch_id: int
    user_id: int
    player_color: str
    opening_key: str
    opening_name: str
    opening_family: str
    opening_score: float
    confidence: float
    coverage: float
    weighted_depth: float
    sample_size: int
    last_practiced_at: datetime | None
    strongest_branch_name: str | None
    strongest_branch_score: float | None
    weakest_branch_name: str | None
    weakest_branch_score: float | None
    underexposed_branch_name: str | None
    underexposed_branch_value: float | None
    computed_at: datetime
```

Constraints:
- `OpeningScoreBatch` is the authoritative snapshot selector
- every `UserOpeningScore` row belongs to one batch via `batch_id`
- reads select rows for the latest batch for `(user_id, player_color)`
- recompute writes the batch row and all score rows in one transaction
- empty recomputes still create a batch row so the cache can represent an honest empty snapshot

### Shared entrypoints

Do not bury recompute logic inside HTTP routes.

Provide reusable cache entrypoints that can be called from:
- session/SRS write hooks
- first-read bootstrap from family/drill APIs
- one-time rollout backfill command
- future imported-game ingestion

Recommended helpers:

```python
def get_latest_opening_score_batch(db: Session, user_id: int, player_color: str) -> OpeningScoreBatch | None:
    ...

def list_cached_opening_scores(db: Session, user_id: int, player_color: str) -> tuple[OpeningScoreBatch | None, list[UserOpeningScore]]:
    ...

def has_opening_evidence(db: Session, user_id: int, player_color: str) -> bool:
    ...

def recompute_opening_scores(db: Session, user_id: int, player_color: str) -> OpeningScoreBatch:
    ...

def ensure_opening_scores(db: Session, user_id: int, player_color: str) -> tuple[OpeningScoreBatch | None, list[UserOpeningScore]]:
    ...
```

Contract for `ensure_opening_scores`:
- if a latest batch exists, return it
- if no batch exists and there is historical opening evidence, compute the initial batch synchronously and return it
- if no batch exists and no evidence exists, return `(None, [])`

That one helper gives downstream APIs a safe bootstrap path without forcing each endpoint to invent its own cache-miss behavior.

### Recompute flow

Recompute all named roots from `get_opening_roots()` for one `(user_id, player_color)`.

Flow:
1. load graph and roots singletons
2. build one evidence overlay for `(user_id, player_color)`
3. compute `RootScore` for every named root
4. create a new batch with one `computed_at`
5. insert all cached root rows for that batch in the same transaction
6. commit once after the batch is complete

Do not mutate the previous batch in place. Write a new batch, then let readers naturally advance to the latest batch.

### Historical backfill strategy

Existing users may already have opening evidence in:
- `session_moves` joined to `game_sessions`
- `blunders` joined to `positions` and optionally `game_sessions`
- `blunder_reviews` via their parent blunders

Without a backfill/bootstrap path, those users would look identical to brand-new users until they make a future write. That is not acceptable.

This issue must include a one-time backfill command, for example:
- `backend/scripts/backfill_opening_scores.py`

The command should:
- enumerate distinct `(user_id, player_color)` pairs with historical opening evidence
- call `recompute_opening_scores()` for each pair
- be idempotent
- support `--user-id`, `--player-color`, `--limit`, `--dry-run`, and `--force`

Candidate-pair discovery should mirror the actual evidence inputs, not just future write hooks. At minimum:
1. from live play: distinct `(gs.user_id, gs.player_color)` from `session_moves sm JOIN game_sessions gs` where `sm.fen_before IS NOT NULL`
2. from ghost targets/reviews without source sessions: distinct `(b.user_id, p.active_color)` from `blunders b JOIN positions p` where `b.source_session_id IS NULL`
3. from blunders tied to sessions: distinct `(b.user_id, gs.player_color)` from `blunders b LEFT JOIN game_sessions gs ON gs.id = b.source_session_id`

Use `UNION` over those sources so rollout backfill matches the overlay logic closely.

### First-read bootstrap

Backfill is required for rollout, but it is not sufficient as the only safety net.

Keep first-read bootstrap in the shared `ensure_opening_scores()` helper so a user with evidence but no batch can still get a real opening response on demand. MVP can do this synchronously inside the request path on the first miss.

This also protects future scenarios where:
- backfill was skipped for one user
- a deploy adds cache tables after data already exists
- external game imports write historical evidence before an async refresh completes

### Freshness hooks

Trigger recompute after the existing opening-evidence mutation points:
- session move uploads in `backend/app/api/session.py`
- SRS review writes in `backend/app/api/srs.py`

These hooks keep future writes fresh. The backfill command and first-read bootstrap cover pre-existing history and any missed refreshes.

### Future import compatibility

Design the cache refresh API so a future chess.com / lichess import feature can reuse it directly.

Expected import flow later:
1. import external games into the canonical evidence tables used by `overlay_evidence()` (`game_sessions`, `session_moves`, `blunders`, `blunder_reviews`, `positions` as needed)
2. collect affected `(user_id, player_color)` pairs from the imported payload
3. call `recompute_opening_scores()` or `ensure_opening_scores()` for those pairs

Do not create a separate import-only opening cache path. Imported games should converge into the same evidence model and the same cache refresh entrypoints.

### Empty-snapshot semantics

For a user/color pair:
- if no evidence exists, `ensure_opening_scores()` may return `(None, [])`
- if evidence exists, the helper should create a first batch rather than returning empty
- an explicit recompute with zero scored roots should still persist an empty batch

This lets downstream APIs distinguish true no-data from cache-not-built-yet cases.

### Tests

Add cache-layer coverage for:
1. recompute writes one coherent batch for one `(user_id, player_color)`
2. two recomputes for the same user/color create two batches and reads select only the latest batch
3. rows from different batches are never mixed in one read
4. `ensure_opening_scores()` bootstraps a batch when evidence exists but no batch exists yet
5. `ensure_opening_scores()` returns `(None, [])` for a truly brand-new user/color with no evidence
6. rollout backfill candidate discovery finds historical user/color pairs from preexisting evidence
7. session upload hook refreshes the relevant user/color snapshot
8. SRS review hook refreshes the relevant user/color snapshot
9. empty recompute creates an empty batch and returns zero rows cleanly

One required test must seed a user with historical evidence but no cache batch and prove that the initial read/bootstrap path creates a usable batch.

### Migration and fixtures

This issue owns all schema and bootstrap work needed for the cache.

Update:
- SQLAlchemy models in `backend/app/models.py`
- Alembic in `backend/alembic/versions/`
- SQLite test schema bootstrap in `backend/conftest.py`
- new script in `backend/scripts/`

### Out of scope

Not owned here:
- family aggregation math
- family-card sorting
- drill-down response shaping
- frontend rendering

Those belong to `g-opening-family-api`, `g-opening-drill-api`, and `g-opening-family-ui`.

title: Add DAG-backed openings children API
description: Implement the backend half of the opening grouping fix by exposing DAG navigation directly instead of relying on family-name prefixes. This issue owns the OpeningRoots traversal helpers, the additive /api/openings/children endpoint, subtree score aggregation, explicit null-last sorting for mixed scored and unscored children, and backend regression coverage.
design:

- Add OpeningRoots.get_descendants(root_key) with per-subtree deduplication across DAG overlaps.
- Add GET /api/openings/children with nullable subtree metrics for unscored children.
- Make backend ordering explicitly null-last for mixed scored and unscored responses.
- Keep existing family endpoints, cache schema, and family derivation untouched.

Required tests
- get_children(None) and get_children(parent_key) coverage
- descendant traversal and shared-node dedup coverage
- Polish single top-level regression
- top-level and drill-down API coverage
- unknown parent_key returns 404
- subtree aggregation math
- mixed scored and unscored sort locks in null-last behavior

Data note
Use the current overlap counts in the plan and tests: 345 roots with multiple immediate parents and 3751 with multiple top-level ancestors.

title: Migrate OpeningsPage to children navigation
description: Implement the frontend half of the opening grouping fix by moving OpeningsPage from family-name grouping to the new /api/openings/children contract. This issue owns drill-down navigation, null and empty-state handling, mixed scored and unscored rendering, and frontend regression coverage.
design:
Scope
- Replace family fetches with getOpeningChildren(playerColor, parentKey).
- Add nav-stack drill-down and back navigation.
- Handle nullable subtree metrics with dash rendering and a No Data tone for unscored cards.

- Keep strongest-first display sorting in the UI while leaving unscored cards at the end.

Required tests

- non-empty true-no-evidence regression: computed_at null and all subtree_root_count values are 0
- non-empty snapshot-empty regression: computed_at present and all subtree_root_count values are 0
