title: Replace stockfish.wasm with stockfish
description: https://www.npmjs.com/package/stockfish
design:
## Goal
Replace `stockfish.wasm` (Stockfish 11, old API) with the `stockfish` npm package (v18.0.7, Stockfish 18 lite, maintained by Chess.com/nmrugg).

## Package comparison

| | stockfish.wasm (old) | stockfish (new) |
|---|---|---|
| Engine version | Stockfish 11 | Stockfish 18 |
| Variant | Multi-threaded (wasm threads) | Lite single-threaded (no pthread) |
| Init | `Stockfish({ locateFile, mainScriptUrlOrBlob })` | `Stockfish({ locateFile, listener })` |
| Send cmd | `engine.postMessage(cmd)` | `engine.processCommand(cmd)` |
| Receive output | `engine.addMessageListener(cb)` | `listener` callback in init options |
| Terminate | `engine.terminate()` | `engine.terminate()` |
| Files | `stockfish.js` + `stockfish.wasm` + `stockfish.worker.js` | `stockfish-18-lite-single.js` + `stockfish-18-lite-single.wasm` |

**Note on threading**: Using `stockfish-18-lite-single.js` (single-threaded). Even though COOP/COEP headers are already set for SharedArrayBuffer, the multi-threaded lite variant requires the pthread worker URL to resolve to itself at runtime, which is complex in Vite's bundle environment. Single-threaded Stockfish 18 lite is still drastically stronger than Stockfish 11. Threads setoption calls must be removed.

## Files to change

### 1. `package.json`
- `npm uninstall stockfish.wasm`
- `npm install stockfish`

### 2. `src/types/stockfish-wasm.d.ts` → rename to `src/types/stockfish.d.ts`
Replace the type declarations to match the new API:
```ts
declare module 'stockfish/bin/stockfish-18-lite-single.js' {
  type StockfishModule = {
    processCommand(command: string): void
    listener: ((line: string) => void) | null | undefined
    terminate(): void
  }
  type StockfishInitOptions = Partial<StockfishModule> & {
    locateFile?: (path: string) => string
  }
  export default function Stockfish(
    options?: StockfishInitOptions,
  ): Promise<StockfishModule>
}
```

### 3. `src/workers/stockfishWorker.ts`
**Import changes**:
- Remove: `import Stockfish from 'stockfish.wasm'`
- Remove: `import stockfishWasmUrl from 'stockfish.wasm/stockfish.wasm?url'`
- Remove: `import stockfishWorkerUrl from 'stockfish.wasm/stockfish.worker.js?url'`
- Remove: `import stockfishMainUrl from 'stockfish.wasm/stockfish.js?url'`
- Add: `import Stockfish from 'stockfish/bin/stockfish-18-lite-single.js'`
- Add: `import stockfishWasmUrl from 'stockfish/bin/stockfish-18-lite-single.wasm?url'`

**`ensureEngine` changes**:
- Add `listener: handleEngineLine` to the init options
- Remove `engine.addMessageListener(handleEngineLine)`
- Remove `mainScriptUrlOrBlob` from options
- Update `locateFile` to only handle `.wasm` (no `.worker.js` case)
- Change `engine.postMessage('uci')` → `engine.processCommand('uci')`

**All `engine.postMessage(cmd)` calls** → `engine.processCommand(cmd)` (~10 calls):
- Covers: `stop`, `ucinewgame`, `setoption name MultiPV value N`,
  `setoption name Hash value 64`, `position fen ...`, `go depth N`,
  `go movetime N`, `go depth N searchmoves ...`

**Remove threads setoption**:
- Remove `pendingEngine.postMessage(\`setoption name Threads value ${threads}\`)`
- Remove the `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` threads computation

### 4. `src/workers/analysisWorker.ts`
Same pattern as stockfishWorker.ts:
- Same import swap (4 old → 2 new)
- Add `listener: handleEngineLine` to init options
- Remove `engine.addMessageListener(handleEngineLine)`
- Replace all `engine.postMessage(cmd)` → `engine.processCommand(cmd)` (~8 calls: lines 61, 84, 91–92, 99–103)
- Remove `setoption Threads` and `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` threads computation (lines 100–101)

### 5. `src/hooks/useStockfishEngine.ts` — remove the SharedArrayBuffer gate
The hook currently hard-fails when `SharedArrayBuffer` is absent:
- `sharedArrayBufferAvailable` module-level const (~line 39)
- Initial `status` state: `sharedArrayBufferAvailable ? 'booting' : 'error'`
- Initial `error` state: `sharedArrayBufferAvailable ? null : sharedArrayBufferError`
- `useEffect` early-return: `if (!sharedArrayBufferAvailable) return`

Since `stockfish-18-lite-single.js` is single-threaded and does NOT require `SharedArrayBuffer`, all four must be removed:
- Delete the `sharedArrayBufferAvailable` and `sharedArrayBufferError` constants
- Initialize `status` as `'booting'` unconditionally
- Initialize `error` as `null` unconditionally
- Remove the early-return guard in `useEffect`

This unblocks:
- `ChessGame.tsx` `allowDragging` (~line 815): was stuck at `'error'` without SAB
- `AnalysisBoard.tsx` engine lines via `useStockfishEngine()`
- `useChessGameController.ts:272`: `evaluatePosition()` was rejected immediately via `status === 'error'`

The `error` state and `'error'` status still exist for genuine runtime failures — only the preemptive SAB gate is removed.

## Testing

The existing test suite (useStockfishEngine.test.ts, useMoveAnalysis.test.ts, GameAnalysisCoordinator.test.ts) mocks `globalThis.Worker` and injects fake messages — it will NOT catch a broken wasm import, bad locateFile path, or a failed boot handshake.

Required verification steps:

### 1. `npm run build`
Mandatory first gate. Catches broken deep imports (`stockfish/bin/...?url`), missing WASM asset, and TypeScript errors from the new API across both workers.

### 2. New smoke test: `src/workers/stockfishWorker.test.ts`
Mock `Stockfish` from `stockfish/bin/stockfish-18-lite-single.js` to return a fake engine object. Verify:
- `processCommand('uci')` is called on init (not `postMessage`)
- A `listener` callback was passed in the init options (not `addMessageListener`)
- The `booted → ready` message sequence propagates to `ctx.postMessage` correctly

### 3. New smoke test: `src/workers/analysisWorker.test.ts`
`analysisWorker.ts` has the same API migration as `stockfishWorker.ts` but its consumer (`useMoveAnalysis.test.ts:17`) stubs `Worker` at the hook boundary and never exercises the real worker path. Same mock-Stockfish pattern:
- `processCommand('uci')` called on init (not `postMessage`)
- `listener` passed in init options (not `addMessageListener`)
- Verify a `ready` message is emitted after the `uciok → isready → readyok` handshake
- Verify an `analyze-move` request results in `position` + `go` commands via `processCommand` (not `postMessage`)

### 4. Updated `useStockfishEngine` test
Add a case asserting the hook starts in `'booting'` state (not `'error'`) when `SharedArrayBuffer` is undefined — confirming the gate is gone.

## Risks
- The `processCommand` method is wired in a `.then()` chain after Emscripten init. If the `await Stockfish(options)` resolves before `processCommand` is set (timing edge case), the first `processCommand('uci')` call would throw. Mitigation: add a `waitForProcessCommand()` poll guard if this occurs in testing.
- File size: `stockfish-18-lite-single.wasm` is ≈7MB vs the old smaller wasm. Increases initial load time but acceptable for an analysis feature.
close_reason: Replaced stockfish.wasm (SF11) with stockfish npm package (SF18 lite single-threaded). Swapped postMessage→processCommand, addMessageListener→listener init option, removed Threads setoption, removed SharedArrayBuffer gate. All 529 tests pass, build succeeds.

title: Evaluate gameplay analysis worker reset latency
Re-show blunder alert with arrows when clicking blunder in MoveList
description: When a user clicks on a move flagged as a blunder in the MoveList component, re-display the blunder alert toast and draw the blunder arrows on the board (showing the player's move vs the best move). This lets users revisit blunder feedback without replaying the entire game.
close_reason: Implemented re-show blunder alert on MoveList click

title: Bug: review pass info box isn't shown
description: In review flow, the repeated-mistake info appears correctly, but the success/pass info does not appear after the player makes the correct move.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player is reviewing and repeats a prior mistake, they should see the repeated-mistake message. When the player then makes the successful/correct move, the success/pass info message should be displayed.
notes:
Observed behavior: when a player repeats the same mistake, they see 'You made this mistake again!'. After they then play the successful move, the corresponding success/info message is expected but not shown.
Implemented frontend fix: show the review pass toast immediately after local pass evaluation, instead of waiting for reviewSrsBlunder API success. Added regression test: pass toast still appears when reviewSrsBlunder rejects.

title: Add pass/fail overlay to Review Position warning box
description: After a 'Review Position' warning, when the user makes their move, overlay a large green checkmark (pass) or red X (fail) covering the entire warning box. The box stays visible until the next user move. If the next position is also a Review Position, stack the boxes: the resolved one (with checkmark/X) on top, the new warning below it.

title: Add triangle warning icon to Review Position warning box
Show ghost target position and SRS data in Review Position Warning
description: In the Review Position Warning, make info available about the position the ghost is steering to, and some basic SRS data (e.g. repetition count, interval, next review date).

title: Frontend: warn at previously failed review position
description: Show a visible warning when player reaches a targeted review position before they move.
close_reason: Added a warning callout when player reaches a targeted review position, plus tests for show/hide behavior.

title: add color background, bigger fonts, for You column
description:
# DESIGN
      
        ## UI Design Spec — Prominent You column in MoveList
      
        ### Current state
      
        • Grid:  2.5rem 1fr 1fr  — both move columns share equal space
        • Container width:  minmax(200px, 260px)  via  .chess-layout  grid
        • Move font: 0.9rem JetBrains Mono,  --text-secondary  color
        • Both columns visually identical — no background, same font size
        • Headers: 0.75rem uppercase,  --text-tertiary
      
        ### Changes
      
        1. Widen MoveList container
      
        •  .chess-layout  grid: change  minmax(200px, 260px)  →  minmax(220px, 300px)
        • Gives ~40px more room for the wider You column
      
        2. Asymmetric grid columns
      
        • Change  grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1fr 1fr  so the You column gets ~60% of the move space
        • When player is white:  2.5rem 1.4fr 1fr
        • When player is black:  2.5rem 1fr 1.4fr
        • Pass a  data-player-col  attribute from the component based on  playerColor
      
        3. Continuous column background tint
      
        • A single continuous vertical band behind the entire player column, not applied cell-by-cell
        (cell-by-cell breaks on split rows and placeholders, reads as broken stripes)
        • Implement via a  ::before  pseudo-element on  .move-list-grid :
          •  position: absolute , full height of the grid
          •  left  and  width  calculated from grid column proportions:
            • Player is white →  left: 2.5rem; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 1.4 / 2.4)
            • Player is black →  left: calc(2.5rem + (100% - 2.5rem) * 1 / 2.4); width: calc((100% - 2.
            5rem) * 1.4 / 2.4)
          •  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, transparent)
          •  pointer-events: none; z-index: 0
        • Grid cells need  position: relative; z-index: 1  so content sits above the tint
        • Requires  position: relative  on  .move-list-grid
      
        4. Bigger font for You column moves
      
        • Player move buttons: bump  font-size  from  0.9rem  →  1rem
        • Player move eval sub-line: bump from  0.6rem  →  0.65rem
        • Engine column stays at current sizes — the contrast reinforces hierarchy
      
        5. You header treatment
      
        • The You header gets  color: var(--text-primary)  instead of  --text-tertiary
        • Slightly larger:  font-size: 0.8rem  (vs 0.75rem for Engine)
        • Optionally: a small bottom accent border  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--brand-secondary)
        replacing the default 1px border
      
        ### Implementation approach
      
        • Add a  data-player-col=white|black  attribute on  .move-list-grid
        • Use CSS attribute selectors to target the correct column:
          •  [data-player-col=white] .move-col-white  gets the player styles
          •  [data-player-col=black] .move-col-black  gets the player styles
        • The continuous tint band is purely CSS via  ::before , no JS or extra DOM needed
        • No new components needed

      
# Implementation Plan: Prominent You Column in MoveList (g-rl79)
   
     ## Context
   
     Both move columns (You/Engine) look identical — same width, font, background. The player's
     column should stand out with a subtle tint, larger font, and header treatment to reinforce
     visual hierarchy.
   
     ## Files to modify
   
     •  src/components/MoveList.tsx  — 1-line JSX change
     •  src/App.css  — ~40 lines of new CSS rules
   
     ## Step 1: JSX — Add  data-player-col  attribute
   
      MoveList.tsx:336  — Add  data-player-col={playerColor}  to  .move-list-grid  div. This single
     attribute drives all CSS changes via attribute selectors.
   
     ## Step 2: Widen MoveList container
   
      App.css:175  —  .chess-layout  3rd column:  minmax(200px, 260px)  →  minmax(220px, 300px)
   
     ## Step 3: Asymmetric grid columns
   
      App.css:2115  — After the base  .move-list-grid  rule, add:
   
     •  [data-player-col=white] :  grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1.4fr 1fr
     •  [data-player-col=black] :  grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1fr 1.4fr
   
     Player column gets ~58% of fr-space, engine gets ~42%.
   
     ## Step 4: Continuous column tint band
   
     Add  position: relative  to  .move-list-grid , then a  ::before  pseudo-element:
   
     •  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, transparent)
     •  pointer-events: none; z-index: 0
     • Position calculated from fr-proportions:
       • White:  left: 2.5rem; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 0.5833)
       • Black:  right: 0; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 0.5833)
   
   
     Grid children sit above  ::before  naturally — no z-index changes on cells needed.
   
     ## Step 5: Player column font bump
   
     Target via  [data-player-col=X] .move-col-X.move-button :
   
     •  .move-button :  0.9rem  →  1rem
     •  .move-eval  (child span):  0.6rem  →  0.65rem
   
     Engine column stays at current sizes.
   
     ## Step 6: Header treatment for You
   
     Use  :nth-child  selectors (headers are 3 spans: eval, white, black):
   
     •  [data-player-col=white] > .move-list-header:nth-child(2)  — You when white
     •  [data-player-col=black] > .move-list-header:nth-child(3)  — You when black
   
     Applied styles:
   
     •  color: var(--text-primary)  (was  --text-tertiary )
     •  font-size: 0.8rem  (was  0.75rem )
     •  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--brand-secondary)  (accent)
     •  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, var(--bg-secondary))  — blends tint
     into opaque bg so sticky header matches column tint without transparency issues
   
     ## Edge cases (all handled)
   
     • Variation lines ( grid-column: 1 / -1 ): span all columns, own background — unaffected
     • Split rows / placeholders: tint covers full column height via  ::before , works regardless of
     cell content
     • Selected move: existing  --card-bg-selected  stacks naturally on top of subtle 6% tint
     • Dark/light mode:  color-mix  + CSS variables are theme-aware
     •  playerColor  changes: attribute is reactive, CSS kicks in immediately
   
     ## Verification
   
     1. Run  npm run dev , start a game as white → confirm You column (left) has tint, bigger font,
     accented header
     2. Start a game as black → confirm You column (right) has the treatment instead
     3. Scroll the move list → sticky header should remain tinted, no visual gap
     4. Check variation lines don't break the layout
     5. Resize browser to verify responsive behavior at smaller widths
design:
## UI Design Spec — Prominent "You" column in MoveList

### Current state
- Grid: `2.5rem 1fr 1fr` — both move columns share equal space
- Container width: `minmax(200px, 260px)` via `.chess-layout` grid
- Move font: 0.9rem JetBrains Mono, `--text-secondary` color
- Both columns visually identical — no background, same font size
- Headers: 0.75rem uppercase, `--text-tertiary`

### Changes

**1. Widen MoveList container**
- `.chess-layout` grid: change `minmax(200px, 260px)` → `minmax(220px, 300px)`
- Gives ~40px more room for the wider "You" column

**2. Asymmetric grid columns**
- Change `grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1fr 1fr` so the "You" column gets ~60% of the move space
- When player is white: `2.5rem 1.4fr 1fr`
- When player is black: `2.5rem 1fr 1.4fr`
- Pass a `data-player-col` attribute from the component based on `playerColor`

**3. Continuous column background tint**
- A single continuous vertical band behind the entire player column, not applied cell-by-cell (cell-by-cell breaks on split rows and placeholders, reads as broken stripes)
- Implement via a `::before` pseudo-element on `.move-list-grid`:
  - `position: absolute`, full height of the grid
  - `left` and `width` calculated from grid column proportions:
    - Player is white → `left: 2.5rem; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 1.4 / 2.4)`
    - Player is black → `left: calc(2.5rem + (100% - 2.5rem) * 1 / 2.4); width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 1.4 / 2.4)`
  - `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, transparent)`
  - `pointer-events: none; z-index: 0`
- Grid cells need `position: relative; z-index: 1` so content sits above the tint
- Requires `position: relative` on `.move-list-grid`

**4. Bigger font for "You" column moves**
- Player move buttons: bump `font-size` from `0.9rem` → `1rem`
- Player move eval sub-line: bump from `0.6rem` → `0.65rem`
- Engine column stays at current sizes — the contrast reinforces hierarchy

**5. "You" header treatment**
- The "You" header gets `color: var(--text-primary)` instead of `--text-tertiary`
- Slightly larger: `font-size: 0.8rem` (vs 0.75rem for "Engine")
- Optionally: a small bottom accent border `border-bottom: 2px solid var(--brand-secondary)` replacing the default 1px border

### Implementation approach
- Add a `data-player-col="white"|"black"` attribute on `.move-list-grid`
- Use CSS attribute selectors to target the correct column:
  - `[data-player-col="white"] .move-col-white` gets the player styles
  - `[data-player-col="black"] .move-col-black` gets the player styles
- The continuous tint band is purely CSS via `::before`, no JS or extra DOM needed
- No new components needed
notes:
# Plan: Prominent "You" Column in MoveList (g-rl79)

## Context
Both move columns (You/Engine) look identical — same width, font, background. The player's column should stand out with a subtle tint, larger font, and header treatment to reinforce visual hierarchy.

## Files to modify
- `src/components/MoveList.tsx` — 2 small JSX changes (data attribute + header class)
- `src/App.css` — ~40 lines of new CSS rules

## Step 1: JSX — Add `data-player-col` attribute
**`MoveList.tsx:336`** — Add `data-player-col={playerColor}` to `.move-list-grid` div. This single attribute drives all CSS changes via attribute selectors.

## Step 2: Widen MoveList container
**`App.css:175`** — `.chess-layout` 3rd column: `minmax(200px, 260px)` → `minmax(220px, 300px)`

## Step 3: Asymmetric grid columns
**`App.css:2115`** — After the base `.move-list-grid` rule, add:
- `[data-player-col="white"]`: `grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1.4fr 1fr`
- `[data-player-col="black"]`: `grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1fr 1.4fr`

Player column gets ~58% of fr-space, engine gets ~42%.

## Step 4: Continuous column tint band
Add `position: relative` to `.move-list-grid`, then a `::before` pseudo-element:
- `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, transparent)`
- `pointer-events: none; z-index: 0`
- Position calculated from fr-proportions:
  - White: `left: 2.5rem; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 0.5833)`
  - Black: `right: 0; width: calc((100% - 2.5rem) * 0.5833)`

Make stacking explicit: `::before` gets `z-index: -1` (negative, within the `position: relative` stacking context of `.move-list-grid`). This avoids relying on subtle paint-order behavior. Sticky headers already have `z-index: 1`; move cells don't need changes since `z-index: -1` on the pseudo is sufficient.

## Step 5: Player column font bump
Target via `[data-player-col="X"] .move-col-X.move-button`:
- `.move-button`: `0.9rem` → `1rem`
- `.move-eval` (child span): `0.6rem` → `0.65rem`

Engine column stays at current sizes. **Note:** On tablet (`minmax(180px, 240px)` at `App.css:3075`), the 1rem bump may cause extra clipping given `nowrap` + `overflow: hidden` on move cells. Treat font bump as optional/nice-to-have — skip it if it causes visible truncation at narrow widths.

## Step 6: Header treatment for "You"
Add a `move-list-header--you` class to the "You" header span in JSX (semantic, avoids fragile `:nth-child` selectors):
- In `MoveList.tsx:340-345`, conditionally add the class based on `playerColor`

Applied styles on `.move-list-header--you`:
- `color: var(--text-primary)` (was `--text-tertiary`)
- `font-size: 0.8rem` (was `0.75rem`)
- `border-bottom: 2px solid var(--brand-secondary)` (accent)
- `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-secondary) 6%, var(--bg-secondary))` — blends tint into opaque bg so sticky header matches column tint without transparency issues

## Edge cases (all handled)
- **Variation lines** (`grid-column: 1 / -1`): span all columns and currently have no background (`App.css:2254`). The `::before` tint will show through behind them. Add `background: var(--card-bg)` to `.variation-line` so the tint doesn't bleed into full-width rows.
- **Split rows / placeholders**: tint covers full column height via `::before`, works regardless of cell content
- **Selected move**: existing `--card-bg-selected` stacks naturally on top of subtle 6% tint
- **Dark/light mode**: `color-mix` + CSS variables are theme-aware
- **`playerColor` changes**: attribute is reactive, CSS kicks in immediately

## Verification
1. Run `npm run dev`, start a game as white → confirm "You" column (left) has tint, bigger font, accented header
2. Start a game as black → confirm "You" column (right) has the treatment instead
3. Scroll the move list → sticky header should remain tinted, no visual gap
4. Check variation lines don't break the layout
5. Resize browser to verify responsive behavior at smaller widths

title: Remove orientation label from home page
